L1.area: Runtime extension modules and their operation handlers

L2.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where certain runtime operations do not enforce required permission and stability checks. Code executing within the sandboxed environment can directly invoke these ungated operations to bypass the permission model, enabling arbitrary code execution even when the necessary dangerous capabilities have not been explicitly granted by the user.

L3.area: Foreign function interface extension module and its operation handlers
L3.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where certain runtime operations for calling native function pointers and reading native memory do not enforce required permission and stability checks. Code executing within the sandboxed environment can directly invoke these ungated operations to bypass the permission model, enabling arbitrary native code execution even when the necessary dangerous capabilities have not been explicitly granted by the user.

ground_truth.reason: The op_ffi_call_ptr and op_ffi_call_ptr_nonblocking operations in the FFI extension were registered without permission type parameters and lacked any permission or unstable API checks, allowing sandboxed code to call arbitrary native function pointers without authorization.
